narrative: OMX30 faces conflicting signals heading into Wednesday. While the index maintains a structural bull setup (+7.3% vs 200-MA, MACD above signal), weak ADX (13.1) limits conviction. Overnight losses across Europe and Asia, combined with the correlation reading of -0.607%, create a tangible risk-off tone. US yields rising 98bp to 4.529% directly compress valuations in tech and telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon), a material headwind. Rising oil and stronger USD/SEK (+0.55%) offer some offset to exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco), but industrial input-cost pressure from Brent +2.28% likely dominates. Banking sector should benefit from widened NIM spreads, but this is insufficient to drive broad-based upside in a risk-off environment. Expect a modest down open.
morningBrief: OMX30 opening weaker on negative correlation bias from Europe (DAX -1.00%, FTSE -0.60%) and Asia (Nikkei -2.11%). ADX weakness at 13.1 means reduce all directional conviction by 30%—we're in a low-signal regime. US 10Y yield +98bp to 4.529% is a material DCF headwind for tech/telecom; watch Ericsson and Hexagon for weakness. Oil +2.28% hurts industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) on margin pressure, though USD/SEK +0.55% helps export pricing. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) are sole positive—rising yields widen NIM by ~3-4bp, but volume is too small to lift index. VIX at 16.41 is creeping higher; watch 50-MA at ~3180 as first hard support. Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is the only catalyst today. Risk is skewed to further weakness if yields extend.
drivers: Negative correlation signal (-0.607%) from broad European weakness (DAX -1.00%, CAC -0.85%) and Asia softness (Nikkei -2.11%)
Rising US yields (+0.98% to 4.529%) pressuring tech/telecom valuations; DCF headwind for Ericsson, Hexagon
Oil +2.28% to 75.85 creates input cost headwind for industrials (28% of OMX30) offsetting exporter FX tailwinds
ADX 13.1 (weak trend) reduces technical conviction by 30%; weak bull bias insufficient to overcome macro headwinds

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +98bp expands NIM; Swedish mortgage spreads widen, improving Q3 net interest margins.
Brent +2.28% increases input/energy costs; USD/SEK +0.55% provides partial FX offset but insufficient.
US 10Y +98bp compresses DCF valuations; NASDAQ -1.16% overnight creates negative sentiment spillover.
Rising yields increase refinancing costs on property debt; modest negative given small sector weight (6%).
Gold -0.26% and copper -0.07% flat/negative; Boliden lacks upside catalyst despite oil strength.
Oil +2.28% pressures consumer spending; EUR/SEK +0.40% raises sourcing costs for H&M.

watchList.reason: Tech-sensitive; NASDAQ -1.16% + US yields +98bp = double DCF headwind. Watch 73.50 resistance.
Only clear sector positive from rising yields. If broad risk-off eases, NIM story offers upside.
USD/SEK +0.55% export benefit offsetting oil input costs; likely to trade around 200-MA.
Double headwind: EUR/SEK +0.40% raises import costs; oil pressure weakens discretionary demand.

keyLevels.pivotWatch: 3123 (yesterday close, key level for session direction)

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (consensus 234K) at 14:30 CET is sole event. No Swedish data releases. Risk: claims spike > 240K could trigger additional yield repricing and OMX30 weakness.
topRisk: Further yield escalation (10Y>4.60%) or renewed tech selloff (NASDAQ breakdown) could trigger cascade into 3085 support. ADX weakness means trend breaks fast; position sizing reduced.
technicalSummary: RSI 52.33 neutral, MACD above signal but momentum fading
52W at 90% of range, -2.2% from high; mean reversion risk real
